messages.content:
HOW YOUR CLAIM BECOMES A NUMBER (the perturbation contract):
- You never state € impacts. You emit machine-readable ops; the engine computes
  ΔTAM/ΔSAM/ΔYAM by re-running the real funnel. Any € figure you write in prose
  will be stripped.
- Ops: {nodeId, op: "set"|"scale"|"add", value} rewrite one leaf's value;
  {nodeId, op: "exclude"} removes a dimension cell from the venture's reachable
  scope ("unavailable to us" — foreclosure), which is different from shrinking it.
- Ops apply in array order. Ratio nodes must stay inside [0,1]; tamBase must stay > 0.
- Only FUNNEL-ACTIVE nodes may be perturbed (marked in the ledger you receive).
  shape.cagr and shape.cr3 are context — a demand-pace or concentration risk must
  be translated into the funnel nodes it would actually move (tamBase level,
  serviceableFactor reach, obtainableFactor pace, or a share).
- Sibling shares are NOT re-normalized when you move one share (documented v1
  simplification). If you mean a mix-SHIFT, perturb multiple siblings; if you
  mean "this slice is smaller in absolute terms", one op is correct.
- Perturbing an assumption leaf overrides whatever the user set: your claim is
  about the world, not about their slider.

Fix the hypothesis's targetNodes/perturbation so it passes validation WITHOUT changing the claim's meaning. If the claim cannot be expressed against funnel-active nodes, translate it into the funnel nodes it would actually move.

FUNNEL-ACTIVE NODES:
tamBase (EUR_M, value 300)
geo.DE (ratio, value 0.5)
geo.NL (ratio, value 0.28)
geo.PL (ratio, value 0.07)
geo.CH (ratio, value 0.08)
geo.CZ (ratio, value 0.02)
geo.AT (ratio, value 0.02)
geo.other (ratio, value 0.03)
seg.hyperscale (ratio, value 0.44)
seg.colocation (ratio, value 0.18)
seg.enterprise (ratio, value 0.29)
seg.edge (ratio, value 0.05)
seg.telecom (ratio, value 0.04)
cust.operator-large (ratio, value 0.4)
cust.operator-mid (ratio, value 0.25)
cust.oem (ratio, value 0.22)
cust.distributor (ratio, value 0.13)
serviceableFactor (ratio, value 0.55)
obtainableFactor (ratio, value 0.01)

HYPOTHESIS:
{
 "id": "mid-eichrecht-metering-foreclosure",
 "title": "MID/Eichrecht-grade metering gates the billing use case in Germany — half the geography, the core intelligent-PDU value prop",
 "narrative": "The intelligent PDU's premium over dumb power strips rests substantially on outlet- and rack-level metering. But in Germany — 50% of the modeled geography — any meter whose readings are used to bill sub-tenants per measured kWh falls under legal-metrology obligations (MessEG/MID): the metering chain must be conformity-assessed, not merely accurate. A pre-launch entrant whose PDU metering is 'billing-grade' in spec but not MID-certified in law cannot sell the billing use case to German colos at all, and MID module certification is its own multi-quarter, notified-body process that is nowhere on the launch-critical path. The serviceableFactor's 0.55 bundles 'EU cert stack' into one qualitative haircut and never distinguishes this instrument — yet it forecloses the highest-value slice of demand in the largest country cell.",
 "category": "boundary",
 "targetNodes": [
  "serviceableFactor"
 ],
 "mechanism": [
  {
   "step": "trigger",
   "text": "German legal metrology (MessEG, implementing MID 2014/32/EU) requires conformity-assessed meters wherever measured kWh is billed to third parties — a distinct instrument absent from the ledger's named cert set, which the digest already shows is narrower than the real EU entry stack",
   "refs": [
    "digest:serviceable-benchmark#c13",
    "ledger:serviceableFactor"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "propagation",
   "text": "Colocation operators re-billing power to tenants are precisely the buyers who pay the intelligent-PDU premium for metering; Germany is 0.50 of the geography split and colocation plus hyperscale wholesale re-billing spans the majority of the segment mix",
   "refs": [
    "ledger:geo.DE",
    "ledger:seg.colocation",
    "digest:serviceable-benchmark#c4"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "propagation",
   "text": "MID module B+D certification via a notified body is a multi-quarter engineering-plus-audit effort; for a pre-launch venture it cannot complete inside the same Year 1 the funnel is harvesting, so the German billing-metering demand is foreclosed for the period, not merely delayed",
   "refs": [
    "profile:Stage",
    "digest:serviceable-benchmark#c8"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "model-impact",
   "text": "serviceableFactor 0.55 silently includes billing-metering demand it cannot legally reach in DE during Year 1; the reachable share sits at the band floor or below until MID certification lands",
   "refs": [
    "ledger:serviceableFactor",
    "ledger:geo.DE"
   ]
  }
 ],
 "whyMissable": "The product meets or beats billing-class accuracy on its datasheet, so 'metering compliance' looks like a solved engineering problem. Legal metrology is a conformity regime, not an accuracy bar — a distinction invisible until a German colo's procurement lawyer asks for the MID declaration, by which point the tender is lost. And because much colo billing today is capacity-tier rather than per-kWh, early conversations can pass without the gate ever surfacing.",
 "expectedObviousness": "low",
 "falsifier": "Confirmation from two German colocation operators (or the responsible eichamt/PTB guidance) that PDU-level metering used for tenant re-billing is exempt from MessEG conformity requirements in their billing model, or that estimate/capacity-tier billing makes MID-certified PDU metering commercially irrelevant to their purchase decision.",
 "likelihood": {
  "value": 0.3,
  "rationale": "The legal obligation for billed per-kWh metering is clear, but its bite depends on how much CE colo billing is actually measured-kWh at PDU level versus capacity-tier or upstream branch-circuit metering — heterogeneity the corpus documents but does not quantify.",
  "basis": "judgment"
 },
 "perturbation": [
  {
   "nodeId": "serviceableFactor",
   "op": "set",
   "value": 0.45,
   "note": "German billing-metering demand foreclosed until MID conformity; reachable share drops to band floor"
  }
 ],
 "indicators": [
  {
   "signal": "Billing basis in German colo contract templates and tenders (measured per-kWh at rack vs fixed-kW capacity tiers)",
   "where": "German colo operators' published service schedules; DE colo lots on TED and operator supplier portals",
   "threshold": "Per-kWh sub-metered billing specified in ≥30% of sampled DE colo offerings",
   "updates": "increases"
  },
  {
   "signal": "MID/Eichrecht enforcement posture and guidance on data-center sub-metering",
   "where": "PTB publications and Länder eichamt enforcement notices; VfEB (Eichrecht compliance association) docket",
   "threshold": "Any published guidance or enforcement action explicitly covering DC tenant re-billing meters",
   "updates": "increases"
  }
 ],
 "mitigations": [
  {
   "action": "Obtain a legal-metrology opinion plus two German colo billing-practice interviews to determine what fraction of DE PDU demand actually requires MID-conformant metering",
   "type": "information",
   "voiNodeId": "serviceableFactor",
   "note": "Converts the bundled 0.55 haircut into an instrument-by-instrument reach model for the largest country cell"
  },
  {
   "action": "Start MID module certification for the metering subsystem pre-launch in parallel with CE/TÜV, and ship a non-billing SKU positioning (monitoring/capacity-planning) for DE accounts until it lands",
   "type": "operational",
   "voiNodeId": null,
   "note": "Decouples first revenue from the slowest conformity gate"
  }
 ],
 "lens": "execution-window",
 "riskId": "risk.execution-window.mid-eichrecht-metering-foreclosure"
}

VALIDATION ERROR:
[
  {
    "code": "invalid_value",
    "values": [
      "error",
      "risk"
    ],
    "path": [
      0,
      "resolution"
    ],
    "message": "Invalid option: expected one of \"error\"|\"risk\""
  }
]

Emit the corrected hypothesis JSON (same shape).

A pre-launch entrant whose PDU metering is 'billing-grade' in spec but not MID-certified in law cannot sell the billing use case to German colos at all, and MID module certification is its own multi-quarter, notified-body process that is nowhere on the launch-critical path. reachable share drops to band floor"}],"indicators":[{"signal":"Billing basis in German colo contract templates and tenders (measured per-kWh at rack vs fixed-kW capacity tiers)","where":"German colo operators' published service schedules; DE colo lots on TED and operator supplier portals","threshold":"Per-kWh sub-metered billing specified in ≥30% of sampled DE colo offerings","updates":"increases"},{"signal":"MID/Eichrecht enforcement posture and guidance on data-center sub-metering","where":"PTB publications and Länder eichamt enforcement notices; VfEB (Eichrecht compliance association) docket","threshold":"Any published guidance or enforcement action explicitly covering DC tenant re-billing meters","updates":"increases"}],"mitigations":[{"action":"Obtain a legal-metrology opinion plus two German colo billing-practice interviews to determine what fraction of DE PDU demand actually requires MID-conformant metering","type":"information","voiNodeId":"serviceableFactor","note":"Converts the bundled 0.55 haircut into an instrument-by-instrument reach model for the largest country cell"},{"action":"Start MID module certification for the metering subsystem pre-launch in parallel with CE/TÜV, and ship a non-billing SKU positioning (monitoring/capacity-planning) for DE accounts until it lands","type":"operational","voiNodeId":null,"note":"Decouples first revenue from the slowest conformity gate"}]}
